claim: Okamoto 1987 (slot 07) §§3+ + appendices contain NO named parameter transformation acting on (v_1, v_2) as (-1 - v_1, v_2); the paper has no appendix and §3 + §4 only iterate ℓ, ℓ̃ and apply existing W^aff(B_2) generators in degenerate strata.

Slot 07 pdftotext extraction is byte-stable: SHA-256 = 2B8576837BE9C212AB28066B4B6FC6B7758D0156544DCADB0FAC028C44AD8B6B; line count = 1144; section headers exhaustively enumerated in extract_okamoto_sec3_pi.md §A.1.

The closest §§3+ candidate to π is Okamoto's s : (v_1, v_2) ↦ (-v_1 - 2, v_2) (§1.3 (ii)); sympy verifies s = π ∘ T_{(1,0)}, equivalently π = s ∘ T_{(-1,0)}, where T_{(-1,0)} is the translation v_1 ↦ v_1 - 1 with v_2 fixed.

T_{(-1,0)} is NOT in Q^∨(B_2): sympy solves (m, 2n-m) = (-1, 0) over integers and finds NO solution; cross-check confirms T_{(1,1)} (=ℓ) and T_{(1,-1)} (=ℓ̃) ARE in Q^∨ (m=n=1 and m=1, n=0 respectively).
T_{(-1,0)} ∈ P^∨(B_2) = Z² but ∉ Q^∨(B_2) = {(a,b)∈Z² : a+b even} (parity check: -1+0 = -1 odd); P^∨/Q^∨ ≅ Z/2 by the standard B_2 = C_2 centre Z/2 of Spin(5)=Sp(2).
π is an involution: sympy verifies π² = id with π(v_1, v_2) := (-1 - v_1, v_2).

The cokernel Z/2 of 033's homomorphism φ : W^aff(B_2) ↪ Aut(D_6^(1)) ⋉ W((2A_1)^(1)) is identified at the lattice-theory level with P^∨(B_2)/Q^∨(B_2): π lives in the extended affine Weyl group W^ext(B_2) = W(B_2) ⋉ P^∨(B_2) but not in W^aff(B_2) = W(B_2) ⋉ Q^∨(B_2).
Outcome (per spec §7): CONFIRM_M6_PHASE_B5_INDEX2_FINAL. 033's INDEX-2 finding is final at the Okamoto 1987 literature level; no STRICT_ISOMORPHISM upgrade achievable from §§3+ readback alone.
